learning_outcomes.description: Have a broad understanding of the fundamentals of nutrition in population health
Understand the rationale for changing food and nutritional requirements during key stages of life to identify the basis of malnutrition across the lifespan

Critically evaluate the influence of socio-political, cultural, economic, and environmental determinants on public health nutrition issues affecting priority populations across domestic and global contexts

Communicate complex concepts related to food systems, nutrition policy, and public health nutrition to both specialist and non-specialist audiences, both individually and as part of collaborative team-based work

Demonstrate advanced reflective analytical and effective oral and written communication skills to identify, curate and critically appraise literature to assess the ethical, cultural, and global implications of nutrition-related decisions

assessments.task_description: Professionals concerned with nutrition should have a clear understanding of the basic principles of, and contemporary issues, in nutrition, and be able to critically review this information. For this assessment, you are required to write about a contemporary public health nutrition topic ( topics will be provided), based on evidence. This will be submitted as a written assessment and will need to be supported with evidence. More detail will be on Blackboard, including a choice of topics. This is an individual assignment.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Please submit on Blackboard following instructions on your task sheet.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
20/09/2026 - 25/09/2026

90 minutes once you begin. The test will be open from 10 AM Sunday 20th September to 2 PM Friday 25th September
assessments.task_description:
This is an individual assessment item, undertaken as an online time-limited activity, and can be done anywhere.

This assessment includes a quiz that will examine content in specific modules. There will be one question which will link to your final assessment.

Please note that your response will be required to provide evidence of critical thinking, and suited to context (location and circumstances). Responses that are copy-pasted from lecture slides or other sources will be given zero marks. Please be aware that AI generated responses are usually very generic, so if you do use AI, please be sure to edit to suit context (location, population and situation) accordingly. Please indicate if you have used AI to assist with your responses.

90 minutes will be allocated for the quiz. You can start at any time on the days the quiz is open, but once you begin, you will be required to complete the quiz.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: SPH Late Submission penalty - 10% per day deduction without an approved prior extension request.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
21/08/2026 - 9/10/2026

Students are expected to complete these reflections on an ongoing basis, rather than at the end.
assessments.task_description: This task is designed to encourage ongoing engagement with lecture content, support consolidation of learning, and provide students with an opportunity to identify key concepts and questions arising from each topic. Students will complete four short reflections across the semester on topic areas of their choice. Entries will be marked once, as a complete set of four, after the final submission deadline — not individually across the semester.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Details will be made available on Blackboard
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Nutrition and health in a chosen location
assessments.due_date:
22/10/2026 - 7/11/2026

Slides due to be submitted by 4 PM Thursday 21 October. Presentations will be online and scheduled during the timetabled lecture session in weeks 12 and 13 where possible, and at a time that is convenient for all students and the course coordinator during these two weeks. It can be held after hours as well during week 12 and 13, if that is the students preferred option. All presentations will be online.
assessments.task_description:
For the final assessment in this course, your task will explore a public health nutrition issue of significance in a particular life stage in a defined social and geographic context (location). This assessment is an oral presentation, supported by evidence.

The oral presentation will define a clear nutrition issue and provide an overview with evidence to support the structural and ecological determinants of the problem or issue. Following the presentations students will also be required to respond to questions. We will arrange timings to suit internal and external students.

Students are asked to use the ecological framework or UNICEF malnutrition framework to discuss the causes and consequences of the chosen nutrition issue, and identify and discuss ONE government-led strategy or program which is currently being applied in that location to address your nominated issue.

A list of topics will be provided along with a detailed task description.

All presentations will be recorded and transcribed for Quality Assurance purposes.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Please submit the slides as a pdf via assessment submission portal.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: SPH Penalty for late submissions at 10% per day applies.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
